sourceDocuments.title: Directive Ashes of Peace Campaign v0.2
Directive Breckinridge Senior Staff Character Bible

ship.name: U.S.S. Breckinridge
ship.openingCondition: Returned to service after a four-month repair and modernization period at Utopia Planitia; certified for service with several upgraded systems still requiring integrated validation under sustained deployment conditions.

commandStructure.playerRole: Principal mission commander and coordinator of shipboard operations

serviceHistory.commissioning: Entered service approximately three years before the campaign.
serviceHistory.wartimeService: Survey, patrol, diplomatic-support, convoy escort, rapid-response, and reconnaissance duty before a major yard period.

yardPeriod.location: Utopia Planitia Fleet Yards, Mars orbit

openProductionDecisions: Exact late-war action that forced the yard period

postRefitWork: Structural repair and recalibration of nacelle articulation
Replacement and recalibration of damaged bio-neural gel packs
Revised tactical software compatible with postwar countermeasure libraries
Warp-field wake monitoring and nacelle-control diagnostics
knownTechnicalDebt: Integrated validation still required under sustained deployment conditions
Command-network certificate compatibility issue exposed during prelude
Refit discrepancies within certified limits but not yet tested together under maximum anticipated mission load

senior.name: Mara Whitaker
senior.packageRole: Strategic authority and final legal command

senior.name: Kieran Vale
senior.packageRole: Gifted test pilot; ambition, mentorship, and controlled risk

senior.name: Priya Nayar
senior.packageRole: Coordination, informal influence, and ethical boundaries

senior.name: Hadrik Bronn
senior.packageRole: Preparedness, discipline, acting-XO handoff, and operational scrutiny

senior.name: Rowan Saye
senior.packageRole: Evidentiary rigor, dissent, and institutional skepticism

senior.name: Miriam Sato
senior.packageRole: Medical reality, bioethics, casualty burden, and honest cost

senior.name: Imani Cross
senior.packageRole: Technical integrity, autonomy, identity, and long-term consequence

cohorts.label: Returning Breckinridge Personnel
cohorts.summary: A small body of returning veterans with institutional memory of the ship's first three years.

cohorts.label: Patrol and Escort Transfer Cohort
cohorts.summary: A large transfer cohort accustomed to wartime readiness, austerity, and direct chains of command.

cohorts.label: Science and Support Transfer Cohort
cohorts.summary: A large transfer cohort accustomed to extended research, civilian coordination, and procedural consultation.

cohorts.label: Individual Specialists
cohorts.summary: Specialists selected for Intrepid-class operations, post-refit systems, or the Asterion assignment.

relationshipModel.startingFrame: The crew has spent twenty-five days underway together; the player is new to the command culture.

mainCampaign.title: Ashes of Peace
mainCampaign.highConcept: The Dominion War is over, but peace has not restored the political order that existed before it. The Breckinridge enters a region where wartime emergency authority, counterfeit Starfleet signals, missing relief cargoes, Cardassian refugees, and Pale Lantern manipulation threaten to turn survival measures into open conflict.
mainCampaign.thesis: Peace is not the absence of an enemy. Peace is the difficult work of deciding what must be repaired, what must be changed, and who is allowed to participate in that decision.
startingDirectives: Restore reliable Starfleet presence in the Asterion Reach.
Protect civilian life and freedom of navigation.
Survey wartime damage to communications, navigation, and subspace infrastructure.
Investigate counterfeit Starfleet signals and missing relief cargo.
Avoid prejudicing ongoing Federation negotiations concerning the Asterion Mutual Aid Compact.
Coordinate with, but not assume direct command over, local civil authorities unless a lawful emergency requires it.

stateTracks.label: Regional Trust
stateTracks.summary: Whether principal factions believe disputes can still be resolved through shared institutions.

stateTracks.label: Lantern Escalation
stateTracks.summary: Pale Lantern integration and progress toward Nightfall.

stateTracks.label: Humanitarian Strain
stateTracks.summary: Shortages, displacement, medical capacity, and civilian exhaustion.

stateTracks.label: Starfleet Scrutiny
stateTracks.summary: Attention from admirals, intelligence offices, legal authorities, and Federation media.

stateTracks.label: Compact Unity
stateTracks.summary: Condition of Compact political cohesion; exact opening value remains a production decision.

chapters.title: A Ship Underway
chapters.question: What kind of executive officer has joined the ship?
chapters.coreConsequence: Initial crew trust and technical debt.

chapters.title: The Empty Convoy
chapters.question: How does Starfleet act when its own authority may be counterfeit?
chapters.coreClue: Authentic code fragments in a false order.

chapters.title: False Colors
chapters.question: Who gets to verify Starfleet's innocence?
chapters.pressure: Public accusation, injured patrol crew, tactical secrecy.
chapters.coreClue: Counterfeit Breckinridge attack routed through Hecate.

chapters.title: Work Worth Doing
chapters.question: What does useful Starfleet presence look like between crises?
chapters.coreReward: Early local assets and crew development.

chapters.title: Dead Letters
chapters.question: Who owns intercepted truth after war?
chapters.pressure: Hazardous relay recovery, privacy, evidence custody.

chapters.title: The Colony That Stayed
chapters.question: Is survival under compromised authority disloyalty?
chapters.pressure: Jurisdiction, collaboration, diverted evacuation, Solenn's culpability.
chapters.coreClue: Local use of Pale Lantern and Starfleet's real wartime failure.

chapters.title: Old Lessons
chapters.question: How should command use experience when the adversary has studied it?
chapters.pressure: Civilian traffic, weapons platforms, authentication theft.

chapters.title: What Survives
chapters.question: Which emergency measures should persist after emergency?
chapters.pressure: Old defenses, trauma treatment, inconvenient science.

chapters.title: The Cost of Knowing
chapters.question: Who may impose secret risk in the name of security?
chapters.pressure: Farwatch authority, evidence purge, false recall.

chapters.title: A Peace of Their Own
chapters.question: What political authority did survival create?
chapters.pressure: Annex occupation, task group, charter negotiation.
chapters.coreResult: The form of regional legitimacy entering the finale.

chapters.title: Before the Lamps Go Out
chapters.question: What should the crew preserve before the final crisis?
chapters.pressure: Memory, scientific hope, identity law, repair.

chapters.title: The Last Directive
chapters.question: Can people cooperate while every system tells them cooperation is dangerous?
chapters.pressure: Command mesh, weapons grid, network quorum, evacuation.

chapters.title: The Terms We Keep
chapters.question: What does accountability require after survival?
chapters.pressure: Settlement, inquiry, public narrative, crew consequence.
chapters.coreResult: Persistent campaign state for the next story arc.

factions.label: Starfleet Reconstruction Command
factions.role: Postwar restoration authority with divided priorities.

factions.label: Asterion Mutual Aid Compact
factions.role: Emergency mutual-aid government whose legitimacy is contested after the war.

factions.label: Compact Security Directorate
factions.role: Hardline security apparatus within the Compact.

factions.label: Cardassian Relief Authority
factions.role: Relief organization caught between need, suspicion, and wartime memory.

factions.label: Project Farwatch
factions.role: Starfleet-linked intelligence project connected to the campaign's compromised infrastructure.

factions.label: Pale Lantern
factions.role: Distributed Dominion contingency system built to weaponize distrust.

sideMissionRules.model: Open Orders intervals contain designed side assignments and shipboard B-plots that inherit current campaign state and commit outcomes back to continuity.

generationPolicy.typicalCompletionCount: 4 to 6 designed side assignments per full campaign

main.title: A Ship Underway
main.missionFunction: Introduce the player to a veteran ship carrying a mostly reconstituted crew and establish the player's command style before the Asterion Reach.

canonEra.voyagerStatus: Voyager remains in the Delta Quadrant and does not intersect the Breckinridge campaign.

missionDirectionRules: Situation on rails, solution off rails.
Every dynamic event must have a causal parent.
Calm side assignments and shipboard scenes may remain unrelated to Pale Lantern.
Crew disagreement should be focused; not every officer gives a moral essay before action.
No character begins with the complete campaign truth.
Move information only when causally justified if the player bypasses an expected scene.
Escalation pacing should serve the situation rather than force combat or countdowns.
Factions act between player scenes based on their goals and information.
failurePolicy: No single failed check ends a mission.
Campaign-critical survival must preserve meaningful continuation after major losses or refusals.
Senior-crew death requires established lethal situation, meaningful decision or accumulated consequence, fair response opportunity, and narrative weight.
Refusing an assignment is a consequential command act, not an out-of-bounds input.
Delay advances clocks only when actors or physical processes have a reason to continue.
